intro-1.plaintext: Container blocks hold other blocks inside them — sliders with slides, grids with columns, accordions with panels. Define them in your blockSchema using blocks_layout or object_list widgets.

children.text: Container blocks hold other blocks inside them — sliders with slides, grids with columns, accordions with panels. Define them in your blockSchema using

bl-text.plaintext: Each child has its own @type and schema. Children are stored in a shared blocks dict on the parent.

children.text: Each child has its own @type and schema (from blocksConfig). Children are stored in a shared
dict on the parent, with the field holding

All blocks_layout fields on the same block share the same blocks dict. So a block can have multiple container fields (e.g., header_blocks and footer_blocks) whose children all live in the parent's blocks.

ol-text.plaintext: All items share one inline schema, stored as an array. Use idField for the unique identifier and optional dataPath for nested data.

children.text: All items share one inline schema, stored as an array:
to an object_list to let items have different types (like blocks_layout) while keeping array storage. Set
to control which field holds the type (defaults to '@type').
Both widgets look the same in the editing UI and blocks can be dragged between them — data adapts automatically when moving between formats.

render-text.plaintext: Add data-block-uid to each child. Use data-block-add for direction hints. Use data-block-selector for paged containers.

children.text: to each child element. You don't need to mark the container element itself:

table-text.plaintext: Set addMode: 'table' for table-like structures so users can add/remove columns as easily as rows.

children.text: for table-like structures (rows containing cells). This lets users add and remove columns as easily as rows:

empty-text.plaintext: A container can never be empty. When the last child is deleted, a defaultBlockType or a special 'empty' block is inserted. Empty blocks are stripped before saving.

children.text: A container can never be empty. When the last child is deleted, either the
is added, or a special block with
is inserted. Empty blocks are stripped before saving. Render them as empty space — Hydra puts a '+' button in the middle for the user to replace it.
